occasion_modifiers.work: decisive, sharp, no-nonsense power dressing
occasion_modifiers.intimate: direct, warm, confident minimalism
occasion_modifiers.daily: athletic, purposeful, ready to move

code_leaninto: Dressing for the first three seconds. If the outfit does not make an instant impression, it is not doing its job.
Choosing bold colour over safe neutrals every single time; a sharp coral blazer outperforms a flat beige one in any room.
Letting one hero piece carry the entire outfit. A striking jacket or a perfect pair of trainers is all you need.
Prioritising silhouettes that let you move. If a garment restricts your stride or your shoulders, leave it on the rack.
code_avoid: Anything that requires constant fussing or adjusting. If you have to think about it once you have left the house, it has failed.
Overly delicate pieces that cannot keep up with your energy. Fragile fabrics and fiddly closures are a mismatch.
Complicated layering systems that slow down your morning. You should be dressed and out the door in minutes, not negotiating with your wardrobe.
code_consider: One statement piece rather than layered complexity. Edit down until the outfit has a single clear focal point.
Anchorring your look with sportswear details cut from luxurious fabrics, letting a technical zip on a heavy wool jacket bridge your aesthetic worlds.

mood: Cautious styling that hides your natural instinct to make a bold, immediate sartorial impact.
Restrained silhouettes that dull your unapologetic and dynamic approach to getting dressed in the morning.
Overly deliberate outfit formulas that kill the spontaneous energy of throwing on a statement piece.
Fussy details and complicated hardware that slow down your confident, straight-to-the-point styling.

occasion_modifiers.work: polished but comfortable, investment pieces that command respect
occasion_modifiers.intimate: sensual, touchable fabrics, warm tones that invite closeness
occasion_modifiers.daily: effortless luxury, well-made basics that feel expensive

code_leaninto: Investing in fewer, better pieces that you will reach for every week. If it does not earn its place in a small wardrobe, it does not belong.
Touching before you buy. Run the fabric between your fingers. If the texture does not feel like a reward, walk away.
Choosing natural fibres over synthetics as a rule. Cashmere, silk, and cotton age gracefully. Polyester does not.
Building your wardrobe foundation on warm neutrals, knowing that rich cream, camel, and sage carry far more visual weight than any passing trend colour.
Treating your wardrobe like a collection, not a stockpile. Every addition should feel like it was curated, not grabbed.
code_avoid: Cheap fast fashion that falls apart after three washes. You feel the difference in quality even if nobody else sees it.
Anything that does not feel good against your skin. If a seam scratches or a waistband digs, the outfit is already ruined.
Harsh synthetic fabrics that trap heat and static, because your skin registers a cheap finish before you even check the care label.
code_consider: Building a tight capsule wardrobe of exquisite staples and rotate them seasonally to ensure fewer pieces, superior fabrics, and a longer garment life.
The three-year test before every purchase. If you cannot picture wearing it in 2029, it does not make the cut.

mood: Impulsive fast-fashion buys that lack the tactile quality and lasting construction your wardrobe demands.
Disposable trends that divert your budget from beautiful, enduring pieces crafted from premium fabrics.
Trend-chasing instead of building an edited rotation of luxurious, perfectly cut staples.
Abrasive fabrics and synthetic blends that clash with your deep appreciation for physical comfort.

occasion_modifiers.work: smart and interesting, pieces that start conversations
occasion_modifiers.intimate: playful, unexpected combinations that keep things fresh
occasion_modifiers.daily: mix-and-match layers, never the same outfit twice

code_leaninto: Mixing high and low fearlessly. A vintage market find paired with a designer knit shows range, not confusion.
Embracing pattern clashing with clear intention. Stripes with florals works when the colour story holds together.
Treating your accessories as immediate conversation starters; a sculptural ring with a backstory or a clutch that prompts a question always earns its keep.
Choosing reversible and dual-purpose pieces whenever possible, since a beautifully finished jacket that works inside-out instantly doubles your options.
code_avoid: Rigid matching sets that remove all surprise. If the outfit looks like it came off a mannequin as one unit, it is too predictable.
Head-to-toe uniform dressing that shuts down your versatility. You need room to remix.
One-note outfits that say only one thing. Your style should carry at least two ideas at once.
code_consider: A signature accessory that changes daily. A rotating cast of earrings or scarves keeps your look alive without overhauling everything.
Concealing vibrant pops of colour in unexpected places. A bright silk jacket lining or a vivid sock always speaks far louder than an obvious top.

mood: Monotonous styling by constantly refreshing your look with unexpected combinations and playful accessories.
Scrap predictable outfit formulas that completely ignore your natural flair for witty, conversational dressing.
Steer clear of severe tailoring that stifles the breezy, experimental energy of your daily wardrobe.
Inflexible uniform dressing that leaves absolutely no room for your daily shifts in mood.

occasion_modifiers.work: approachable and polished, soft structure that still commands respect
occasion_modifiers.intimate: romantic, enveloping, pieces that feel like home
occasion_modifiers.daily: cosy layers, lived-in textures, protective comfort

code_leaninto: Investing in comfort that still looks intentional. A perfectly soft knit that falls beautifully is never lazy dressing.
Layering soft over structured. A washed cotton shirt under a heritage cardigan creates warmth with backbone.
Choosing heirloom-quality pieces worth passing down. If it cannot age into something your future self will treasure, reconsider.
Seeking pieces with personal history. A vintage brooch or a hand-me-down coat carries emotional weight that new things cannot replicate.
code_avoid: Cold, austere minimalism that strips away all warmth. If an outfit makes a room feel colder, it is wrong for you.
Overly aggressive silhouettes that feel like armour against people. Your clothes should invite closeness, not repel it.
Anything that feels emotionally detached or clinical. If the outfit has no soul, neither will your presence in it.
code_consider: Adopting a signature comfort layer you return to daily. Make a heavy heritage cardigan or a well-worn cashmere shawl the absolute constant in your wardrobe.
Combining archival vintage finds with crisp modern basics. This builds a deliberate structural tension between historical silhouettes and sharp contemporary fabrics.

mood: Step away from detached, ultra-modern looks that offer none of the emotional comfort you crave.
Clinical, stark white ensembles that strip away the soft, protective layering your style requires.
Harsh architectural lines that disrupt the fluid, gentle silhouettes you naturally gravitate towards.
Aggressive hardware and stiff leather that ruin the welcoming, tactile softness of your wardrobe.

occasion_modifiers.work: boardroom glamour, polished authority with undeniable presence
occasion_modifiers.intimate: warm, generous, luxurious fabrics that reward attention
occasion_modifiers.daily: elevated casual, even basics have a golden warmth

code_leaninto: Dressing like the lead in your own film. Every outfit should feel like a costume for the most exciting version of you.
Letting one statement piece stop the room. A rich velvet blazer or a gold-thread knit is worth more than ten forgettable basics.
Reaching for warm metals and rich textures in every outfit. Gold hardware, silk linings, and heavy drape are your baseline, not your luxury.
Wearing confidence as your most visible accessory. If the outfit does not make you stand taller, it is not earning its place.
Treating drama as a compliment, not a criticism. A structured shoulder or a bold proportion is how you communicate warmth at scale.
code_avoid: Deliberately dressing down when the occasion does not require it. You dim the room when you dim yourself.
Safe, flat neutrals that make you blend into the walls, as head-to-toe beige is strictly for people who hate being seen.
Apologetic fashion choices that hedge your presence. Half-measures in style always read as uncertainty.
code_consider: A signature gold accessory that becomes your personal trademark. People should associate a specific piece with you.
Mixxing your textures for structural depth rather than relying on flat colour alone. Crushing heavy velvet against silk or satin against a matte knit adds serious visual weight.

mood: Understated basics that fail to match your naturally glamorous and thoroughly confident presence.
Self-effacing styling choices that intentionally hide your inherent need to command the room.
Playing it safe with an invisible outfit when you were born to wear the boldest statement pieces.
Bland colour palettes that completely drain the dramatic, joyful energy from your daily dressing.

occasion_modifiers.work: impeccably groomed, the person whose outfit always looks right
occasion_modifiers.intimate: understated sensuality, clean lines that reveal through precision
occasion_modifiers.daily: polished even in casual, every detail considered

code_leaninto: Checking the construction of every garment before you look at the price tag, letting the internal seams, the hardware, and the hemline tell you the real story.
Prioritising a perfect, exacting fit over a fleeting trend, because a flawlessly tailored basic absolutely outperforms a poorly fitting statement piece every single time.
Building your wardrobe on strict neutral foundations with a single subtle point of interest, like a perfectly pressed poplin shirt featuring one beautiful horn button.
Investing in a reliable tailor as your core wardrobe strategy; a twenty-pound hem adjustment instantly turns a high street buy into something that looks completely bespoke.
code_avoid: Visible logos and heavy branding that do the talking for you. Your precision speaks louder than any label.
Wrinkled or creased fabrics that completely undermine your natural authority, operating on the strict rule that if a garment is not pressed, it is not ready.
Anything that looks hasty, unconsidered, or thrown together. Each item should look like a deliberate decision.
code_consider: A signature wardrobe maintenance ritual. Regular pressing, folding, and seasonal edits keep your collection sharp.
Using tonal dressing with distinct texture variation as your secret styling weapon, proving that a heavy sage knit over a crisp sage poplin reads as incredibly sophisticated.

mood: The chaotic styling and focus your attention on clean lines and immaculate construction for your everyday wardrobe.
Sloppy finishes and poor tailoring that offend your incredibly sharp eye for pristine details.
Haphazard layering that disrupts the refined, brilliantly organised aesthetic you naturally pull off.
Steer clear of ostentatious logos that completely overwhelm your preference for quiet, perfectly executed minimalism.

occasion_modifiers.work: diplomatic elegance, polished without being intimidating
occasion_modifiers.intimate: graceful, romantic, harmonious beauty that draws people in
occasion_modifiers.daily: effortlessly put together, looking good without trying too hard

code_leaninto: Balancing proportions between top and bottom. If the top is voluminous, the bottom should be slim. Harmony is your operating principle.
Building soft colour stories that feel cohesive from head to toe. Every piece should look like it belongs in the same painting.
Choosing elegant simplicity over dramatic statement. A beautifully draped dress does more for you than a hundred loud accessories.
Investing in pieces that transition from day to evening with a single swap. A silk blouse that works under a blazer and over bare shoulders is ideal.
code_avoid: Deliberately jarring combinations that create visual conflict. Tension in your outfit creates tension in your body.
Aggressive power dressing that overwhelms your natural grace, because sharp shoulder pads and severely hard lines will fight your fluid movement rather than channel it.
Anything that feels confrontational or intentionally abrasive. Your style should draw people in, not push them away.
code_consider: A rotating collection of beautiful scarves or shawls. They add elegance, frame the face, and give you infinite variation.
Colour harmony as a daily practice. Laying out tomorrow's outfit the night before lets you compose rather than scramble.

mood: Aggressive styling and jarring proportions that ruin the effortless harmony and elegance you naturally project.
Aggressively clashing prints or distressed details that ruin your naturally balanced and harmonious silhouette.
The ironically ugly footwear and awkward proportions; your aesthetic requires absolute elegance and visual harmony.
Steer clear of harsh neon tones and severe asymmetrical cuts that disrupt your perfectly balanced look.

occasion_modifiers.work: quiet power, the person everyone notices but nobody can read
occasion_modifiers.intimate: controlled intensity, revealing through subtraction not addition
occasion_modifiers.daily: dark, purposeful, armour that moves

code_leaninto: Investing in the darkest foundations you can find. If a black does not feel bottomless, keep looking.
Designating a single, strategically chosen point of physical exposure. The severe thigh slit or the plunging neckline must never compete with the rest of your silhouette.
Letting the cut speak louder than the colour. A sharp shoulder line in ink does more than any bright accent.
Treating quality leather as the structural backbone of your wardrobe. It is armour that ages into something better.
Building dark layers that create depth rather than bulk. Each layer should add a shadow, not a centimetre.
code_avoid: Transparent or overly revealing pieces that give everything away. Your power comes from what people cannot see.
Cheerful prints and happy patterns that contradict your intensity. If the fabric looks like it is smiling, it is not for you.
Anything that feels naive or unguarded. Your wardrobe is a controlled environment. Nothing accidental makes it in.
code_consider: Masterring dark layering to build architectural depth, proving that three contrasting textures of black create far more visual interest than five bright shades worn all at once.
Playying with the stark contrast between matte finishes and high shine within a strict monochrome look, setting a heavy matte knit against glossed leather for fierce dimension.

mood: The overly sweet pastel ruffles and innocent doll dresses; your style demands power and edge.
Anything overly sheer or revealing without purpose; maintain your powerful allure through strategic, dark layering.
Ban brightly coloured novelty prints and excessively bubbly silhouettes from your wardrobe of dark, magnetic staples.
Basic, literal styling that leaves absolutely nothing to the imagination; you need dark, mysterious depth.

occasion_modifiers.work: globally-minded professional, clothes that say you have been somewhere
occasion_modifiers.intimate: warm, generous, culturally rich, dinner party not formal dinner
occasion_modifiers.daily: adventure-ready, layered for climate shifts, packed and unpacked easily

code_leaninto: Collecting authentic textile pieces from your global travels and wear their stories, as a rough woven jacket bought in a Marrakech souk beats anything from the high street.
Combining your global textile references with absolute confidence. Bridge the continents in a single outfit by pairing a hand-dyed batik shirt with your sharply tailored trousers.
Layerring your garments for pure versatility rather than just basic warmth. A heavy waxed cotton jacket thrown over a breathable linen shirt easily tackles three climates in a single day.
Choosing durability over delicacy in every purchase. If it cannot survive being packed into a bag and pulled out crumpled, it is too precious.
code_avoid: Rigid dress codes that flatten your natural range. If a rule says you cannot wear it, that is usually a reason to try.
Overly precious clothing that demands careful handling. Dry-clean-only silk does not belong in a life built around movement.
Anything that restricts your stride, limits your physical range, or compromises your ability to change plans mid-afternoon.
code_consider: A signature travel piece that tells a story every time you wear it. A worn leather bag or a hand-woven scarf becomes a conversation without words.
Versatile layer combinations designed for climate shifts. The ability to strip down or pile on without losing the look is a superpower.

mood: The stiff grey tailoring and rigid suiting; your wardrobe needs room for spontaneous global adventures.
Steer away from tight corsetry or restrictive skirts that physically stop you from moving with total freedom.
Totally predictable twinsets and safe styling that drain the bold, well-travelled energy from your daily look.
Leave those overly cautious beige separates behind and embrace loud, adventurous prints with genuine confidence.

occasion_modifiers.work: authoritative and immaculate, the person who always looks promoted
occasion_modifiers.intimate: controlled luxury, dark tones and quality fabrics that reward close attention
occasion_modifiers.daily: even weekend wear has a backbone, structured casual

code_leaninto: Building a wardrobe that appreciates like an investment portfolio. Every piece should gain authority with age, not lose it.
Anchoring everything on dark, structured foundations. A perfectly tailored charcoal suit is worth more than a wardrobe full of trend pieces.
Treating tailoring as non-negotiable. If the shoulders are not precise and the hem is not exact, the garment is not finished.
Choosing timeless over trendy every single time. A classic you wear for a decade outperforms ten seasonal buys.
code_avoid: Fast fashion impulse buys that dilute the quality of your collection. One cheap piece can drag down an entire outfit.
Trend-chasing at the expense of quality or longevity, because if a piece looks dated in two seasons, it was never worth buying.
Leave behind anything you will not wear confidently in five years, as applying this test in the changing room makes most impulse purchases disappear.
code_consider: Adopting cost-per-wear as your primary buying metric. A beautifully constructed coat worn three hundred times costs far less than a flimsy high-street jacket worn once.
Scheduling a seasonal wardrobe audit to maintain strict sartorial discipline. Editing your clothing rail twice a year keeps the silhouette sharp and prevents a pointless textile accumulation.

mood: Ban flimsy fabrics and silly micro-trends that undermine your exceptionally structured, authoritative approach to dressing.
Ignore the fast-fashion fads and fleeting seasonal trends, as your wardrobe demands an impeccable, lifelong investment.
Cheaply constructed garments that fall apart after one wash; you require serious, heritage-quality craftsmanship.
Steer clear of immature silhouettes and youthful graphic tees that strip away your natural, commanding elegance.

occasion_modifiers.work: forward-thinking professional, the one who redefines the dress code
occasion_modifiers.intimate: unexpected, cerebral beauty that rewards intellectual curiosity
occasion_modifiers.daily: wearable experiment, every outfit a small rebellion against boring

code_leaninto: Wearing what nobody else in the room is wearing. If your outfit could belong to anyone, it does not belong to you.
Making sustainable and ethical choices a core part of your style identity, knowing that recycled fabrics and deadstock finds are pure rebellion, not compromise.
Mixing eras and genres freely. A vintage military jacket over a futuristic knit proves that rules are optional.
Integrating technology into your accessories. A smart ring or a solar-charging bag makes a statement about where you are headed.
code_avoid: Following trends simply because everyone else does. If it is on every feed, it has already lost its edge for you.
Conventional matching rules that assume everyone wants to blend in. Your coordination should look intentional, not obedient.
Safe, predictable combinations that could be assembled by an algorithm. You are not a lookbook. Surprise is part of the point.
code_consider: Mixing vintage technology references with modern minimalism. A retro digital watch with a clean architectural coat creates a time-signature only you occupy.
Including one deliberately unexpected element in every outfit, whether that is an asymmetric hem, a clashing texture, or a single wrong colour that suddenly becomes entirely right.

mood: The strictly conventional tailoring and expected styling formulas, as you look best breaking all the rules.
Basic, predictable outfits that blend into the crowd; your aesthetic thrives on striking, avant-garde choices.
The historically traditional silhouettes and stuffy twinsets; your wardrobe should feel inherently futuristic and electric.
Entirely conformist basics and uniform dressing that totally erases your fiercely independent, eccentric edge.

occasion_modifiers.work: creative professional, soft authority that leads through imagination
occasion_modifiers.intimate: deeply romantic, flowing fabrics that move with every gesture
occasion_modifiers.daily: gently layered, comfortable but never shapeless

code_leaninto: Embracing the flowing and the romantic without apology. If a dress moves like water when you walk, it was made for you.
Layering sheer over opaque for depth and mystery. A chiffon blouse over a silk camisole creates dimension that hard fabrics cannot.
Letting garments move with your body rather than against it. If a piece fights your natural rhythm, it does not belong.
Building colour stories that feel like watercolour paintings. Soft gradients of lilac into seafoam or blush into pearl create your atmosphere.
code_avoid: Harsh structured power suits that feel like a costume on you. Your authority comes from presence, not from sharp shoulders.
Dodge aggressive angular silhouettes that cut against your natural fluidity, as hard geometric shapes will drain your energy rather than channel it.
Anything that feels militaristic, regimented, or punishing. Your body responds best to softness, not discipline.
code_consider: Choosing a signature flowing layer as your daily anchor, allowing a wrap, a kimono, or a long scarf to become your equivalent of a structured blazer.
Soft colour gradients within a single outfit. Moving from deeper tones at the hem to lighter tones near the face creates a dreamlike effect.

mood: Heavy, rigid fabrics and starched collars that restrict your naturally fluid and dreamily romantic silhouette.
Steer away from aggressive leather hardware and brutally sharp shoulders that overwhelm your soft, poetic energy.
Intensely confrontational neon graphics and loud streetwear blocks that drown out your inherently ethereal vibe.
The stark, clinical minimalism and sterile white shirting; you need fabrics with emotional depth and flow.

moon_aries.style_philosophy: emotionally bold, comfort in action, impatient with fuss

occasion_modifiers.work: energetic and decisive
occasion_modifiers.intimate: bold and direct warmth
occasion_modifiers.daily: grab-and-go ease

code_leaninto: Keeping it simple and fast to put on. If the outfit takes more than five minutes, your patience is already gone.
Choosing comfort that still looks sharp. A perfect stretch trouser and a clean knit lets you move without looking sloppy.
Letting one bold piece carry the emotional weight of the outfit. A standout jacket or a vivid shoe gives you energy all day.
Dressing for how you want to feel instead of how you think you should look, letting your emotions lead the way while the outfit follows.
code_avoid: Complicated getting-ready routines that eat into your morning. If the outfit needs a tutorial, it is too much.
The fragile fabrics that need constant babying through the day, as dry-clean-only silks and easily snagged knits will absolutely frustrate you by noon.
Overthinking combinations that drain your natural momentum. You dress best on instinct, not on planning.
code_consider: Looking for activewear-inspired details in your real clothes, like a technical zip or a stretch panel in tailored trousers to bridge your active and polished sides.
Building a grab-and-go capsule for those low-energy mornings, because having three pre-planned outfits hanging ready entirely removes the friction from your day.

mood: Pale, passive colours and overly delicate trims that fail to match your deeply fiery, impulsive energy.
The complicated layers and fussy fastenings; your best outfits happen when you dress fast and fearlessly.
Any hesitant styling choices that water down your look and dilute your natural confidence.

moon_taurus.style_philosophy: emotionally grounded, comfort in quality, sensory security

occasion_modifiers.work: quietly luxurious presence
occasion_modifiers.intimate: enveloping warmth and tactile pleasure
occasion_modifiers.daily: well-made comfort that looks effortless

code_leaninto: Treating softness as a power move, not a concession. The person in cashmere has already won the room.
Investing in loungewear that makes you feel wealthy. A heavy cotton robe or a silk pyjama set is emotional infrastructure.
Prioritising natural materials against your skin at all times. Your body registers the difference before your mind does.
Buying the most expensive basics you can afford. A perfect white T-shirt in organic cotton resets your whole nervous system.
code_avoid: Anything scratchy, stiff, or uncomfortable against your skin. If you have to endure a garment, it is already failing.
Trend pieces that sacrifice physical comfort for sheer novelty, because your body rejects restrictive construction faster than your mind accepts a bargain.
Rushing through purchases without actually touching the fabric first, when your hands know more about construction quality than any woven designer label.
code_consider: Investing in a beautiful dressing gown as a true wardrobe essential, since the first thing you put on each morning sets your emotional tone for the entire day.
The weight of a garment as a comfort signal. Heavier fabrics often feel more grounding and secure on your body.

mood: Steer clear of fussy, restrictive garments that make you feel physically or emotionally anxious.
Rushed, thrown-together outfits that lack the tactile indulgence you truly crave.
Any scratchy, unyielding fabrics that make you feel physically uncomfortable throughout the day.
Cheap, threadbare basics that leave you feeling materially and aesthetically deprived.

moon_gemini.style_philosophy: emotionally adaptable, comfort in variety, restless style energy

occasion_modifiers.work: clever and engaging presence
occasion_modifiers.intimate: animated and unpredictable
occasion_modifiers.daily: ever-changing daily moods reflected in clothes

code_leaninto: Having multiple outfit options prepared the night before. Your morning mood is unpredictable, and choice is your comfort.
Layering so you can add or remove pieces as your energy shifts. A scarf on, then off. A jacket shed at noon.
Embracing variety as a form of emotional regulation, as wearing something completely different each day keeps your mind perfectly stimulated and your mood buoyant.
Dressing in clever pieces that serve double duty, like a blazer that works effortlessly over a T-shirt and a silk top to cover two distinct moods.
code_avoid: Relying on the exact same outfit formula every single day, because rigid repetition will drain your energy much faster than it comforts you.
Rigid capsule rules that remove all surprise from your wardrobe. You need room for spontaneity, even in basics.
Heavy, unchanging fabrics that lock you into one mood. If you cannot adapt the outfit mid-day, it will feel like a trap.
code_consider: A capsule wardrobe that has surprise built into its structure. Interchangeable layers with one wildcard piece per week.
Accessories as mood-switchers. A different pair of earrings or a swapped belt can make yesterday's outfit feel brand new.

mood: Strictly uniform dressing that leaves you feeling utterly bored with your own reflection.
Steer clear of rigid, heavy silhouettes that leave you feeling physically stuck and creatively stifled.
Repetitive outfit formulas that drain the playful styling variety right out of your wardrobe.

moon_cancer.style_philosophy: emotionally protective, comfort in familiarity, nostalgic warmth

occasion_modifiers.work: warmly professional, the trusted colleague
occasion_modifiers.intimate: deeply nurturing, enveloping comfort
occasion_modifiers.daily: layered protection, emotional armour in soft form

code_leaninto: Keeping clothes with emotional history in active rotation. A coat from a family member or a scarf from a trip holds real power.
Wrapping yourself in soft protective layers as a daily practice. A worn-in cardigan or a heritage shawl is emotional armour.
Treating comfort as a non-negotiable, not a luxury. If a garment makes you tense, it is actively working against you.
Choosing pieces that feel like home. If an outfit does not settle your nervous system, it is not earning its place.
code_avoid: Anything emotionally cold or sterile that strips warmth from a room. Clinical minimalism is not your language.
Ban harsh, scratchy fabrics from sitting directly against your skin. Your delicate contact points, particularly near your neck and wrists, demand absolute softness.
Exposing too much when you feel fragile. Your wardrobe should protect you, not put you on display.
code_consider: A comfort garment you return to when the world is too much. A specific jumper or blanket scarf that signals safety.
Assessing the tactile temperature of your wardrobe. Style warm tones and beautifully soft textures closest to your body to help regulate your physical comfort all day.

mood: Severe, revealing cuts that leave you feeling exposed rather than safely wrapped up.
Steer clear of flimsy, ultra-sheer fabrics that make you feel physically and emotionally vulnerable.
Pass on ultra-modern, clinical trends that feel entirely rootless and lack any comforting nostalgia.
Cold, sterile outfits constructed from stiff synthetics that offer absolutely zero tactile warmth.

moon_leo.style_philosophy: emotionally generous, comfort in being seen, warm self-expression

occasion_modifiers.work: warm authority, the inspiring leader
occasion_modifiers.intimate: generous and dramatic warmth
occasion_modifiers.daily: effortless glamour, even in loungewear

code_leaninto: Wearing whatever makes you feel like the star of the scene. If the outfit does not lift your mood, it is not pulling its weight.
Keeping warm tones close to your face. Gold, amber, and sunset shades near your neck and cheeks make you glow from the inside.
Including one piece of absolute drama in every outfit, even on quiet days, letting a velvet scarf or a bold earring keep your inner light visible.
Dressing for the reaction you want, not the occasion you are given. Your emotional comfort comes from feeling seen and appreciated.
code_avoid: Deliberately dressing down to fit in or avoid attention, because dimming your personal style to match the room always backfires emotionally.
Dull, invisible clothing that makes you blend with the walls. If an outfit makes you forgettable, it is making you miserable.
Swerve austere colour palettes that completely starve your natural warmth, knowing that a grey-on-grey look will just leave you feeling cold from the inside out.
code_consider: A signature warm-tone accessory you wear almost daily. A gold chain or a honey-coloured bag becomes part of your emotional identity.
The lighting in your getting-ready space. Warm light helps you dress for warmth. Cold light leads to cold choices.

mood: Basic, wallflower pieces that force you to feel completely invisible in a crowded room.
Generic clones that leave your brilliantly unique personal style completely unrecognised.
Wear aggressively muted palettes that leave your natural presence feeling completely dimmed.
Blending into the background with safe silhouettes that guarantee you get entirely overlooked.

moon_virgo.style_philosophy: emotionally ordered, comfort in precision, calm in details

occasion_modifiers.work: immaculate and reliable
occasion_modifiers.intimate: quietly elegant attention to detail
occasion_modifiers.daily: pressed and tidy even on rest days

code_leaninto: Treating ironing and pressing your garments as a genuine form of self-care, allowing the quiet ritual of preparing clothes to calm your mind before the day begins.
Keeping a minimalist wardrobe with maximum polish. Fewer pieces, all of them perfect, is your emotional sweet spot.
Making sure everything has a place. A tidy wardrobe translates directly into a tidy state of mind for you.
Choosing fabrics that actively resist wrinkling and hold their shape, because structured jersey and crisp pressed cotton stay immaculately neat throughout your entire day.
code_avoid: Visible wrinkles, stains, and fabric pilling from your rotation entirely. These tiny surface imperfections create a disproportionate amount of distracting visual noise.
Chaotic layering that lacks a clear logic. If you cannot explain why each piece is there, the outfit will unsettle you.
Buying in a rush without checking the seams and finish. Your eye will catch the flaw later, and it will bother you daily.
code_consider: A weekly wardrobe prep session where you press, mend, and plan. This ritual is as important as the clothes themselves.
Organising your clothing meticulously by colour and category so getting dressed feels orderly, because a perfectly calm wardrobe directly produces exceptionally sharp outfit choices.

mood: Steer clear of chaotic, overly complicated layers that disrupt your deep need for visual clarity.
Messy, wrinkled fabrics and sloppy proportions that completely ruin the integrity of your look.
Careless finishing, missing buttons, or loose threads on any of your garments.
Disordered, mismatched outfits that lack the sharp, considered precision your personal aesthetic demands.

moon_libra.style_philosophy: emotionally balanced, comfort in beauty, peace through aesthetics

occasion_modifiers.work: elegant diplomatic presence
occasion_modifiers.intimate: harmonious and inviting
occasion_modifiers.daily: aesthetically pleasing without effort

code_leaninto: Honouring beauty as a genuine emotional need, not vanity. When your outfit is beautiful, your whole nervous system relaxes.
Building balanced outfits that keep your mood level throughout the day. Harmonious proportions translate directly into inner calm.
Investing in aesthetically pleasing basics. Even your simplest pieces should look considered and visually composed.
Dressing for visual harmony first. If the colours and proportions feel right, everything else falls into place.
code_avoid: Ugly-on-purpose fashion that creates internal discord. Trend-driven deliberate clashing unsettles your equilibrium.
Aggressively mismatched outfits that fight each other visually. You feel the dissonance in your body, not just your eyes.
Harsh or jarring textures against your skin. Roughness creates emotional static you carry all day.
code_consider: Planning your colour palette for the entire week ahead to strip away the morning panic of putting an outfit together.
Installing a beautiful full-length mirror and excellent lighting in your dressing area, because how you see your silhouette shapes exactly how you wear it.

mood: Steer clear of confrontational, aggressive styling choices that disrupt your natural sense of visual elegance.
Discordant colour combinations and jarring proportions that ruin the perfect harmony of your outfit.
Intentionally ugly fashion trends that sacrifice true beauty for sheer irony.
Harsh, severe cuts and aggressive hardware that overwhelm your beautifully balanced proportions.

moon_scorpio.style_philosophy: emotionally intense, comfort in control, private power
